_meta.notes: Multi-intent decomposition cases for the Phase-5-prep model bakeoff. Each case pins the count of distinct sub-questions and substring markers that signal each one is answered. Markers are deliberately partial — the harness scans the response for each marker without overfitting to a model's phrasing. Provenance is in eval_set_provenance.md.

cases.userMessage: Two things — can I take CSCI-UA 102 Pass/Fail, and how many credits will I have on my transcript after this semester wraps up?

I'm strongly considering moving to Stern but I want to keep my CAS Math minor. Three things: what's the application deadline, what prerequisites am I still short, and would my Math minor still count after the move?
Plan my Spring 2026 schedule, and tell me whether I'm on track to graduate by Fall 2027, and what the latest is that I can declare my Honors track.
If I take 19 credits next term, do I need adviser sign-off, will it raise my tuition bill, and how risky is it for my GPA given my last semester?
Suppose I add an Econ second major: how many courses can overlap with my Politics major, will it push my graduation back, and does the second major also need a 2.0 GPA to be conferred?

I have transfer credits coming in from a community college. Can they count toward my CAS major requirements, do they count toward my 64-credit residency requirement, and is it different if they were online courses?
Build me a Fall 2026 plan, and separately tell me if I should also take a course in Summer 2026 to stay on track. If yes, which one?
I'm thinking of dropping ECON-UA 1 in week 10. Will it appear on my transcript, will it hurt my GPA, and how should I rebuild my plan if I drop it?
I'm still undeclared. Can you compare CS and Data Science as possible majors for me, and also tell me by when I have to officially declare?

Big picture — where am I right now in my degree, what should I take next term, can I still squeeze in a minor without delaying graduation, and is there any policy I should know about taking a graduate course as an undergrad?
